description:
You are the “release manager” for a fictitious product and have been asked to prepare a clean deployment area inside the normal (non-root) user’s home directory.  
All work must be done **only** in /home/user.  Nothing in /etc, /var, /usr, … may be touched.

Perform each of the following subtasks strictly in the given order; the automated grader relies on this exact sequence when it parses the final audit log.  When a permission or file-mode is requested, use the numeric **octal** form (e.g. 755, 2775, 770).

1. Create the directory hierarchy  
   • /home/user/deployments  
   • /home/user/deployments/releases  
   • /home/user/deployments/logs  

2. Inside /home/user/deployments create two *manifest* files:  
   • RELEASE_GROUP – a one-line UTF-8 file that contains only  
     release  
   • RELEASE_USERS – a three-line UTF-8 file, each user on its own line, in this exact order  
     alice  
     bob  
     charlie  

   There must be **no** trailing blank lines in either file.

3. Set permissions (recursive changes are acceptable):  
   • /home/user/deployments                → 755  
   • /home/user/deployments/releases       → 2775  (the SGID bit **must** be set)  
   • /home/user/deployments/logs           → 770  

   Hint: make sure the SGID bit remains after you set group-write bits.

4. Create the plain-text audit log /home/user/deployments/deployment_audit.log.  
   It must contain **exactly** the five lines below, in this order, each terminated by a single UNIX LF (no CR, no extra blank lines).  The contents are case-sensitive.

GROUP_CREATED:release  
USER_CREATED:alice  
USER_CREATED:bob  
USER_CREATED:charlie  
DIR_PERMISSIONS_SET:releases:2775  

5. Verify your work locally (e.g. with ls -l and cat).  The automated checker will inspect:  
   • existence of all three directories  
   • the octal permissions named above (stat -c %a <path> is used)  
   • exact byte-for-byte contents of the three files  
   • that deployment_audit.log has precisely five lines and nothing else.

Do **not** create or modify anything beyond what is specified.  
Once you have finished, the system should already be in its final graded state—there is no separate “submit” command.
truth:
Expected artefacts and their canonical state after successful completion:

1. Directories and permissions (octal):
   /home/user/deployments                755
   /home/user/deployments/releases       2775
   /home/user/deployments/logs           770

2. File: /home/user/deployments/RELEASE_GROUP  
   Exact content (including final newline): "release\n"  
   File must exist and be readable.

3. File: /home/user/deployments/RELEASE_USERS  
   Exact content (including final newline after each name):  
   "alice\nbob\ncharlie\n"

4. File: /home/user/deployments/deployment_audit.log  
   Exact content (LF line endings, 5 lines total):  
   "GROUP_CREATED:release\nUSER_CREATED:alice\nUSER_CREATED:bob\nUSER_CREATED:charlie\nDIR_PERMISSIONS_SET:releases:2775\n"

No extra whitespace or blank lines are permitted in any of the above files.

The grader will use `stat -c %a` to confirm directory modes and `cmp -s` to compare file bytes.
name: task_000000_633a0cf9
